reviews.review_text:
SHADOW MONEY WIZARD GANG.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
WE LOVE CASTING SPELLS.
How deep does it go? When does it end? What does the cauldron do? How many boundaries do I break? What are the eyes? What do they mean? What does any of this mean? Am I even casting spells? What is Noita? Does this game even exist?

...

Haha funny explosion wand go brrr
Good game! 10/10
in the noit club straight up noiting it, and by it, haha, well lets justr say, my noit
my friend told me he lost his 1.5 hour playthough because he used a spell and it turned him into oil. after that i bought the game.

Works great on Linux. 
It's fun and frustrating, time flies when you're exploring and tinkering. 
This feels like it firmly belongs in the golden age of gaming, when they were made by people who enjoy playing around and making the impossible possible.
One of the best games ever made! It's really good and I love it, definitely a favorite. The only thing is that I hate playing it.

40 hours currently and i still haven't even SEEN the final boss. i first got it because i thought the idea of a physics based pixelated roguelike is supercool (and trust me it is) then later i find out that there's a damn mountain of content in this game. so much to explore. if you love a game where you will never figure it all out just by playing this is perfect
I keep coming back to Noita. It is my comfort game. Not quite sure that that says about me ;)
I absolutely suck at this game. I can't get my brain to work in it and I just can't seem to improve. But... I love it so... idk if that says more about the game or me or what. 10/10

Really wanted to like this game, but it's just so incredibly vindictive towards the player. I get it's supposed to be hard... but there's difficult and then there's trying to punish the player. And the punishment isn't just dying over and over and over and over and over... and over. It's that you HAVE to go back to the Mines. 85% of my time was spend in the mines, and so even though I saw all these really cool videos about people doing cool stuff... I did none of it. This was after 55 hours playing! After 55 hours, I feel like you should be able to experience something other than level 1... but the community has Stockholm Syndrome, and they will tell you that you need like, 500 hours minimum before you can do anything. That's insane, and highly ridiculous.
This game has me in the most frustrating bind. On one hand, it's great. On the other, I just blew my sht smooth off with a plasma blast and now I'm raging.

Play this game if you love the feeling of dark souls. only difference is that here you blame yourself rather than the game.
This game makes your greed look like the greed that the bible was talking about. If you even dare to take one step in the wrong direction it could be the end of you. And 99.9% of the time you die the only one you can blame is yourself. Which makes the game especially annoying because after you die on a banger run and is on the death screen contemplating your life choices. you know deep within the only one to blame for that loss is yourself. for a physics like game the amount of times you even have a chance to blame the game because of your death is minimal. trough my 458 runs in this game i can say maybe 4 times i can blame the game for my death and even then it was i who got myself in that situation to begin with. if you hate your innerself and want to see your greed and then pay for that greed by getting humbled by a physics engine operating purely on your choices you have made the right choice.

I have never seen a game that encourages creativity and 'breaking' the world to this extent before. There is always more to explore, more to learn, and genuine secrets that people have yet to figure out. This is the most replayable and exciting roguelike I have played and if it clicks with you... oh boy does it click. It's not for everyone, certainly, and some of the secrets are a bit obtuse, but I won't say they're unfair, I'm just an idiot. I definitely recommend getting this if you can, give it a shot!

One of the best games I've ever played and encourages creativity.

My only issue with the game is that it forces me to play in Windowed mode else I suffer from memory leaks, crashing either my game or PC after only 10 minutes.

Even with all this, I highly recommend it, and I hope we get a Noita 2 some day.

I don't know how I am just now playing this game. If you like roguelikes and like sandboxes, you will love this game. Watching footage of it doesn't really do it justice. I'm only a few hours in and I can't stop thinking about this game.
noita is fun to explore and experiment with.

This is an absolute BEAUTY of a video game. 
The mechanics is extremely extremely rewarding unlike most modern rougelike.
The customization you can do this game requires you to be knowledgable in the game mechanics, which ties back to the game's central theme in plot beautifully. 
You can destroy everything in the game. And make the craziest thing I have ever experienced in a video game. Try it.
Playing this feels like being in an abusive relationship with a boxer, and I love it.

A fantastic game with truly no equal on the current market. Unmatched in terms of potential complexity, creativity, and depth of the world. 

At times it feels like you need a PhD in Noita to explain certain interactions or figure out why something happened.

At some point you, the player, will feel like an alchemist hunting the philosopher stone, trying to play this game. You'll find some wiki page which links you to a youtube video with 4k views explaining complex quantum mechanics as to how chainsaw wrapping works, which then links you to another random website that no other website links to. That page will have the exact wand build you need, but it's written half in finnish and half by what sounds like a raving lunatic. Trying to understand some areas, you'll stumble upon diagrams and maps that were scribbled together some years ago and, while slightly outdated, nobody else except the original author really knows why it works the way it does, so nobody wants to make new ones. And, occasionally if you watch streams of the game in chat, a random chatter named "Ballface33345" will explain some unique quirk in the engine that nobody has ever written down before. 

You will not experience the pinnacle of this game until you have spend dozens of hours learning mechanics, tricks, quirks, experimented on your own. Then, suddenly, you make some sort of discovery. You check all the wikis and nobody has mentioned it. You search online and nobody else has discovered it. At that moment, you will understand the game. You will have found your own technique. Now you can write your own guide, your own map, your own wiki page, for the future adventurers to read and learn from. That is when you will know what the game is about it. That is when you can feel at peace.

There is always something new to find. There is always a new rare seed to discover. There is still more to understand about the game, and there are still more secrets to solve. 

It's a game you have to give time to. It's brutally difficult, but also mostly brutally fair. It's a learning process. But it is incredibly rewarding once you can consistently win runs. It took me about 50 hours before I won my first game. Have fun with it. Highly recommend to any rogue-like enjoyer.

There is nothing quite like Noita.  It succeeds both as an unforgiving little game that you can pick up for a few hours on a weeknight, and as a highly complex ([i]highly[/i] complex) puzzle that rewards you for becoming hopelessly ([i]hopelessly[/i]) obsessed with it.

You remember when Elden Ring came out and everyone simultaneously experienced those [spoiler]trap chests that teleport you to Caelid and the Altus Plateau,[/spoiler] and the feeling was something like "this game is HOW BIG?". Well, Noita kind of does something similar.... over and over and over again.

Also.  This is the best-sounding game I've ever played.  It is number one.  Shoutout Niilo and friends.  Shoutout Finland.

a roguelike in the purest sense. it has the charm of old-school dungeon crawlers with weird creatures and eldritch lore, and WAY too many secrets for its own good. there's a reason people have made careers out of youtube channels just playing and analyzing the game for years...

stupidly unfair, random, and breakable, with a vast vast world

yup Noita is again one of those game you need to put everything aside to play it,pain and a lot of death awaits the player,you will maybe think iam joking right, im not, iam always serious when it come video game and this one is like being punch on balls,
have fun.

Fun game. Hard game. Many many many ways to play it, with many things hidden around the worlds. GL. If you struggle with damage, it would be recommended to use the Spell Lab mod. It may help in understanding.

Would I recommend this game to other players? No, not most people honestly, I think a large percentage of players would quit and never play again after the first hour, and I wouldn't blame them, but if you're the right type and you commit to seeking knowledge whatever the cost, there's much to seek.
It feels like the principals of hermetic alchemy were a design guide for the gameplay, making it a very unique piece of art, and there's infinite emergent gameplay, mostly to kill you, but among it there's a lot more to do than you would think.
Discovering everything this game has to offer alone would likely take lifetimes, but it's best to stay with as few spoilers for as long as you can, the discoveries are worth it.
Great game. Go in blind and expect to die frequently. A very complex system awaits if you pay attention.
Made me go from a pirate to a buyer, this game almost fulfills everything that I was looking for in a pixel-art roguelike and I have no regrets purchasing it. 10/10
The most wonderful and beautiful game. Never ending chaotic alchemy fun!
Do this game up! its a rouge like game. You will die many many times. Sometimes in truly amazing ways. Occasionally you'll get doom wands that level everything... including you! Enjoy~!

Honestly I can't even give a full review yet. Even after 22 hours I haven't beat the final boss. This game just keeps showing me new interesting content that I never even knew it had before play. Honestly best 20 dollars I have spent this year.

start fresh run 
pour out my ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ potion (pheremone)
"ooh a chest right at the start!"
it has the lightning stone inside of it
remember i'm in a liquid (pheremone that i poured out at the start
stone rolled far enough to where i cant grab it and die less than 30 seconds after i start
i love this game
this game is demanding and intimidating as hell. the physics and also the ♥♥♥♥ you can make/combine is really cool but it's just too hard for me i think

Honestly one of the best single player games out there. It took a long time to get a win for me (i got greedy every god run and tried for more) but its so worth it. got first win at 350 hours and first sun quest a couple games later. its such a good game with so much replayability  with good achievement markers and no help along the way. im looking forward to seeing the end of achievements with the eye quest some day hopefully. but either way its 570 hours worth playing!

if you're a masochist this game will satisfy you

this is genuinely the most fun rage i've ever been in

im in a euphoric angry fervor
Good visuals and gameplay, but pretty much a rage game at some points.

It's so deep, so hard and sooo good.

"Beating" the game let's you experience probably at most 10% of the game, and then the rest requires you to break the rules. 

And the devs ENCOURAGE you to break all the "rules" you encounter. Limited spells? Lmao. Checkpoint collapsing after you leave? Only if you don't have one of 20 ways to escape them. World boundaries with huge damage? It's only huge when you even take any damage or don't have infinite health. Every step of the way you can exploit spells and enemies, and even when you do, and eventually become a god, you can still die very easily if you get reckless.
difficult, and rewarding. not for those easily frustrated.
I love Noita, I like to just go around blowing stuff up. W sandbox game.

Starts run, dives down hole, finds a rod that shoots sawblades shotgun styled, gets cornered by monsters, sawblades ricochet into me, dies. 10/10 can't wait to die to oil fires.

(Serious note, this game has a ton of funny moments and great mods!)
My game data just got wiped after all these hours. Lost my crown and everything I am not placing the damn suns again.

This game is great.  It's frustrating at times, a grind, and hilarious ends to your journey keep you coming back.  It's a little bit addictive, sometimes so much so that you swear yourself off it for the night only to find yourself booting up a new game an hour later.  I haven't beaten it yet, not even close.  But seeing the amount of stuff you can do once you get into late game, end game and after with the quests makes me keep on going.  Strongly recommend.  It's a good time.  Get Noita'd with me.
to understand noita, you must be either a genius or unemployed
very complex and in-depth rouge-like gameplay and brutal at the same time
A deceptively simple looking game that have tons of depth to it. you cold sink hundreds of hours into this game and each new run will still feel as fun as your first.

This game has a steep learning curve, and everything is  trying to kill you. That makes surviving and managing a good run infinitely more rewarding than it is in easier games. Good for those who enjoy roguelikes, survival, souls-like, exploration, and secrets
really great game, but use mod to tweak the difficulty

great game, worth the money just for the main game, secrets unincluded
beware though this game is HARD
Game says "♥♥♥♥ you" to your face every 15 minutes, this game is hard and as wide and deep as an ocean. Well worth the money.

The game is rather enjoyable, but not as much as seen in trailers.

We have a lot of options for spell components, but since dying is happening a lot and it's quite random, we can't really prepare an elaborate composition to cast. Most of the time we have to pack everything into one wand or choose a simple, cheap spell and spam it everything we see a monster. It's either playing safe and actually leveling, or playing with explosions only to die randomly on 1st or 2nd floor.

But that's the only issue. On a positive note, art team delivered beautiful and complex animations, pixel art is stunning and the fluid/smoke physics are just genius. They're without doubt the best systems I've encountered in 2D productions.

158 hours in and I still feel like I dont understand anything. Great game for people who dont mind dying for the sake of discovery and understanding. Its super chaotic. You will either feel like an untouchable god who reshapes the law of nature to his will or a pitifull surviving worm who just wants to get another health refill.

I love Noita, its super addicting with a near unlimited amount of replayability. simple yet complex wand building mechanhics, and gameplay to hook you in and explore deeper. with plenty of corners, bosses and secrets to give you hundreds of hours of fun. highly recommend if you like figuring games out by your self. because Noita will not hold your hand! or if your a fan of other games like Enter the gungeon or any roguelite/like. 10/10 masterpiece.

honestly the game is very good other then a few bugs, like if you hit the stalitites the wrong way the top of it will fall instead of the part that is supposed to fall
still a good game though

Noita is a complex game that is not what it seems on the surface, although it can be frustrating at times, for example getting one tapped by a random object after having an amazing run.. but that is what you should expect from Noita.
I can't really say much without either spoiling or not letting you have the full experience.
The only bad thing are the constant crashes for no reason at all.

It's probably not for everyone as it could be frustrating at times. But after 5 hours I can tell the there is something deep and that I just scratched the surface here. The only criticism I have is that this game might require more time to master than I actually have to dedicate to it.

Just so you know, this game has a challenge to collect 34 Collectibles, the last of which can be obtained legally either with a 0.0000001% chance, or with the help of seed hunting and third-party programs. At the end of the 12-hour run, standing on one specific pixel and praying that the game doesn't crash or your computer doesn't fry, and all that just to receive as a reward 1 red pixel on your character.

This will explain what exactly this game is.
Only after playing this game do you feel a need to fulfil your god complex as much as possible. Having that said, 10/10 would recommend.

This game is excellent for what it is! You will start by playing a fairly good platform game, and as you get better at it you will start to find ways to exploit the mechanics. Yeah, no, you didn't. You are REQUIRED to do those things! Expect to die a lot.
Are you the target audience? Do you like Liero, Nidhog, Souls-likes, Rogue-likes, Finnish alchemical fan fiction? Then yes
I cannot beat this game as despite making a 13 spell long A+ stat having wand I cannot handle the exaggerated swagger of true finish game design

It's a cute lil pixel game where you play as a Noita, which is Russian for witch. You use your russian witch spells to fight zombies but uh oh...everything's on fire. You did it. There's goop monsters that spit acid, so you fire more spells at them! Uh oh! More fire! You find more wands that do more things, make bigger fires! You keep going down. Down down to goblin town! Then you hit ice world. All that fire comes in handy. Then it's plant world. Everything's on fire now! You burn up so good, and you start again.

Fun game.
game good game run well game difficult(me masochist so this good) game epic

I have genuinely never seen a more jank mess of a game try to screw me over more in my entire life...and yet it's addicting.

You will die to the most insanely dumb ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, think "♥♥♥♥ I'll never get a run that good"...only to do something even dumber the next run.

And to top this burning mess off, there is so much stuff in this game to interact with. I haven't been playing for long, but in every run I AM ALWAYS LOST!

Seriously, this chaotic mess is 100% worth it
Cracked version worked ok, game was cheap enough that I've decided to buy it rather than pirating again when I wanted to play Noita.
This game reminds me everytime that I do suck,I have always sucked,and will continue to suck.
I've been highly captivated by this game due to it's wonderful interactivity, element complexity, soundtrack, and fun.

One of the prime examples of a game that follows the "haha funny simple premise with obscure and niche mechanics that require entire wikis to document." While it is technically a roguelike, you could legitimately consider improving your knowledge of the game with each death or miscast an upgrade.

Also, you can trip balls hard enough that reality itself shifts to accomodate.  No, I'm not joking.
One of our favorite games we always come back to. Can you make it multiplayer in a shared screen kind of diablo ps one set up? Imagine 4 people trying to coordinate... what are you guys working on?
People are saying what 100 hours of noita does to a person. But it doesnt even take 3 hours to make someone go insane from playing this game. Winning in this game will eat away at your life

You're going to die and die again but you'll also enjoy... most of it.

Super cool roguelike with an incredible physics/elemental interaction system.  Highly reccomended if it looks like the sort of game you'd enjoy.
Amazing game. Full of mysteries and extremely rewarding when you figure out the mechanics.

This isn't a game, this is hell. You come home tired after a long day, you turn on the game, you think, "I'm safe, it's my time" NO! This game will rob you of your time, your self-esteem, your friends, and your life's purpose. You will experience the purest agony, dying over and over again for hours. 
10/10
this game is incredibly neat but I am bad at it and will never finish it. Highly recommend it though, me not finishing it is just a me problem and a skill issue
An incredibly interesting & atmospheric game where every pixel is destructible. It's kind of like if Worms was an atmospheric metroid-vania. Which sounds like it shouldn't work but it does.

Noita is one of the most beautiful roguelites I’ve ever played, equal parts wizard simulator and physics experiment gone horribly wrong.

Every run starts with hope and ends with me turning myself into a fine red mist because I got impatient, mixed the wrong spells, or decided that testing my new wand in a cramped mineshaft was a good idea.

The game doesn’t punish you so much as calmly document your poor decisions.

Cause of death: Stupidity.

10/10. I will absolutely do the exact same thing again.

This is literally one of the best games I have ever played in my life and it's no hyperbole.
You can just get into a run whenever you feel like, you don't have to find your way back into the game like with big, story heavy single-player games BUT the game also offers the "deepness" of games like those if you want to dig deeper.
The "create your own spell" system is incredibly fun, complex and never gets boring.
The environment and every pixel being simulated is great and the difficulty is perfect, if it would be too easy, the game would lose all of its "meaning". Reminds me of Rain World in that aspect.
You can unlock so much stuff and find so many secrets it's insane! The difference between just going for the finish and finding out about everything else is like...
it feels like you think you finished a game but then you realize that this was just one area out of like 10 and you basically just finished the tutorial.
game good. also its funny to me that only 9.9% of players have gotten a victory (I am not one of the winners)
Difficult, but a super fun and deceptively deep roguelike with thousands of hours of potential

so simple on the surface but the true rouge-like elments of the game mkae it much deeper. its tough but there not a lot to lose and if your quick and lucky you fal into these quick loops where you constantly blaze through levels, die, restart. Super fun.

The game is cool, using different strategies and experiencing crazy things. But the problem is: the game is overly Hard! For real, you go trough long journeys just to die at the end by some small error or a strong enemy (and return to the beginning)…
But even considering that the game is still worth it, Very well made and unique

I studied Spells, Alchemy, Ancient Texts. I mix lava with ambrosia, I can create infinitely propagating spells, near-alive wisps to do my bidding, I travelled through parallel worlds, I can destroy heaven and earth with a single spell and survive, I can create literal mini-stars. Yet, I could only manage to turn the world to gold.

A world both deceptively deep and deceptively wide. 29 hours in, I still feel like I've barely scratched the surface of the thing. It's much, much bigger than just "Powder Toy: But Wizards"

There are some annoyances, but they're so minor compared to the fun of the rest of the game, even in (constant, repeated) death, that I don't foresee myself stopping any time soon.

Side note: absolutely don't look things up in the wiki. Find explicitly spoiler-free tips, at most. This is a game for experimentation above all.

Noita is one of those games that feels almost impossible to describe to someone who hasn't played it. Every single pixel in the world is simulated, which sounds like a tech demo gimmick until you realize it actually shapes how you play. Water douses fire, lava melts steel, blood pools and freezes, and your own spells will absolutely kill you if you get reckless with them.

The wand crafting system is where the game truly shines. It starts off looking like a simple roguelike spell setup, but the deeper you go, the more you realize you're basically programming weapons of mass destruction. Some of the most satisfying moments come from cobbling together a wand that completely breaks the game in your favor, right before it ironically blows you up.

What really keeps Noita special is how much it refuses to explain itself. There are secrets layered on top of secrets, parallel worlds, hidden bosses, and lore puzzles that the community is genuinely still unraveling years later. Brutal, beautiful, endlessly creative, and easily one of the most original roguelikes ever made. Also it became a must install on my Steam Deck!

This game respects you as a player, doesn't hold your hand while giving secrets and clues on what to do.

Very solid gameplay loop (you do 1 loong run, learn something new, die, take a break). 

Eventually you hit a jackpot and your run is all about not choking. I'm assuming that more experienced players can manage with bad rng too. It's SO HARD, I love it
Every pixel is out for blood ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ perfect difficult game and I'm only in the tutorial as I've heard.
I'm addicted. Oh god, I'm addicted to this game. This is definitely my favorite roguelike game I've ever played. It checks all my boxes. So ridiculously detailed, you can't stop learning more.

11/10. Quite literally my favorite game of all time, no contest.

Art style, music, gameplay, tinkering + builds, exploration, replayability - you could just keep going, this is going to get 10/10 on every item in the list. Oh, and loads of amazing mods. Addictive, fascinating, and delightfully weird.

Simply cannot recommend enough.

Noita is a very special game. Some say it's the best single player game of all time. Go in blind or read guides, whatever you choose to do the game is a blast to play. And after finishing the base game and all the quests/pillars, there's lots of great mods to further enhance your Noita experience. Sometimes I boot up Noita just to try different wand builds, sometimes I just speedrun it. So much fun! 
Oh and the Noita community is full of weirdos and weebs, loving it!!
totally addicted to this game, its fun to explore the caves and upgrade the wands, even if they get me killed most of the time, 49 hours in and ive barely scratched the surface 10/10

If 3D had never been invented, and 2D game design just kept evolving and getting more complex... Noita is what would happen.  And it did.  And it is the most satisfying game to beat I have ever beaten.

I'm gonna play this too much aren't I. this one breaks away from most action rogues released in it's time frame and both catches me and keeps me in a way I haven't felt since like, spelunky hd. similar beginner feeling too where playing it is way more simple than it looks.
also it's very comfortable on controller, only gripes are your standard bumper item changing instead of number keys and inventory management feels a bit bad though you won't really be messing with that unless you're safe anyway (unless unless you pick up the wand tinkering perk or have the mod which starts you with it turned on.)
Very enjoyable game! Steep learning curve, but once you understand it well, a run only takes about an hour.

This is the most frusturating BS unpredictable game you will ever play and it will make you feel very upset at times, and yet i'd go as far to say it easily makes its way into the top 3 roguelikes of all time and is without a doubt the greatest wizard wizard game ever, in terms of gameplay systems and depth at least. Every single pixel is simulated and there are a bunch of random substances so you'll very often see yourself dying to sheep juice or acid or something. The worst thing in this game, in terms of difficulty, is the fire. For the love of god everything is on fire you need to learn to use the potion you get at the start of the run because everything randomly sets on fire for any given reason all the time. I've heard the roguelike aspect of it is only a tiny fraction of the actual game and that there is an overral progression, but instead of basing it off permenant changes it is largely based on your own knowledge, it's more of an open world wizard sandbox with a roguelike minigame that just so happens to take a million hours to complete.
it's so much fun to play and die to some random thing that explodes everything or trying a wand that looks powerful but backfires so hard you laugh at how absurd it is. 10/10 game

cool wizard man gaming.

love the magic effects, the difficulty is all over the place but it is fun to replay, i really love the wand magic combination to make a broken power to destroy the enemies faster, really fun roguelike i recommend!
Excellent game for people who love customization and modification of weapons.
Hands down the best game I [i] will [/i] ever play. 150 hours and still haven't reached the boss of the tutorial.
this game is like the dark souls of Dungeon Crawler really hard but fun only tip i have is expect everything to kill you.

Wiki Game that requires you to spoil it for yourself to play at a reasonable level. 

People who read spoilers say they hated having ruined the game for themselves. Other people advise me to install cheats to circumvent difficulty. Honestly disappointed by what looked like an amazing game that can't decide how it wants me to play it, but all choices are wrong.

Seems to enjoy specifically obtuse mechanics that a single player could never feasibly discover on their own; probably the next best way to discovered how to play would be to watch a streamer, something I've never been into personally

Why does the game punish players for exploring? Every time I found a secret, I got slapped so hard, I felt like I was doing something wrong by daring to have a curiosity, which forced me back to the "main path" and eventually dying again about 2-3 portals down

If you get into this sort of thing, you'll love it, but I have too large a backlog of games that feel like respecting my time to make room for one that patently has none
RNG so bad it's a masochist game. F&H levels of bs, yet this is not supposed to be depressing like F&H. Everything else is okay.

Will definitely ruin your life. Worth it. 

My mind has expanded. I now have wandcrafting trivia in place of the memory of my mother's face and my bank PIN. Worth it.

I understand how Add Trigger works. My wife has left me. Worth. It.
玩了8个小时没有一次是被怪打死，每次都是踩到某个像素点就突然暴毙。肉鸽内容确实有趣，但是每次装备刚刚有些起色，就tm踩到一两个像素点的陷阱然后死了。

Kicked the minecart that spawned in the first room, that minecart beat the ♥♥♥♥ out of a bunch of enemies and left me gold nuggets, and that gold led me to a chest full of more gold. 10 enemies with a suspiciously minecart-shaped injury out of 10.
I am bad at beating this game, but this game is good at beating me. It's awesome, play it NOW
